verses.kjv: And it came to pass after these things, that the butler of the king of Egypt and his baker had offended their lord the king of Egypt.
verses.ct: Some time later, the cupbearer and the baker of the king of Egypt committed offenses against their master, the king of Egypt.
verses.web: After these things, the butler of the king of Egypt and his baker offended their lord, the king of Egypt.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh was wroth against two of his officers, against the chief of the butlers, and against the chief of the bakers.
verses.ct: Pharaoh became angry with his two officials — the chief cupbearer and the chief baker.
verses.web: Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief cup bearer and the chief baker.

verses.kjv: And he put them in ward in the house of the captain of the guard, into the prison, the place where Joseph was bound.
verses.ct: He had them locked up in the house of the captain of the guard, in the same prison where Joseph was held.
verses.web: He put them in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, into the prison, the place where Joseph was bound.

verses.kjv: And the captain of the guard charged Joseph with them, and he served them: and they continued a season in ward.
verses.ct: The captain of the guard assigned Joseph to attend to them, and he took care of their needs. They remained in custody for some time.
verses.web: The captain of the guard assigned them to Joseph, and he took care of them. They stayed in prison many days.

verses.kjv: And they dreamed a dream both of them, each man his dream in one night, each man according to the interpretation of his dream, the butler and the baker of the king of Egypt, which were bound in the prison.
verses.ct: One night, both the cupbearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were confined in the prison, each had a dream, and each dream carried its own meaning.
verses.web: They both dreamed a dream, each man his dream, in one night, each man according to the interpretation of his dream, the cup bearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were bound in the prison.

verses.kjv: And Joseph came in unto them in the morning, and looked upon them, and, behold, they were sad.
verses.ct: When Joseph came to them the next morning, he noticed that they both looked troubled.
verses.web: Joseph came in to them in the morning, and saw them, and saw that they were sad.

verses.kjv: And he asked Pharaoh’s officers that were with him in the ward of his lord’s house, saying, Wherefore look ye so sadly to day?
verses.ct: He asked Pharaoh's officials who were with him in his master's prison, "Why do your faces look so distressed today?"
verses.web: He asked Pharaoh’s officers who were with him in custody in his master’s house, saying, “Why do you look so sad today?”

verses.kjv: And they said unto him, We have dreamed a dream, and there is no interpreter of it. And Joseph said unto them, Do not interpretations belong to God? tell me them, I pray you.
verses.ct: They replied, "Each of us had a dream, but there is no one here to explain what they mean." Joseph said to them, "Don't interpretations belong to God? Please, tell me your dreams."
verses.web: They said to him, “We have dreamed a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it.” Joseph said to them, “Don’t interpretations belong to God? Please tell it to me.”

verses.kjv: And the chief butler told his dream to Joseph, and said to him, In my dream, behold, a vine was before me;
verses.ct: So the chief cupbearer described his dream to Joseph. He said, "In my dream, there was a grapevine right in front of me."
verses.web: The chief cup bearer told his dream to Joseph, and said to him, “In my dream, behold, a vine was in front of me,

verses.kjv: And in the vine were three branches: and it was as though it budded, and her blossoms shot forth; and the clusters thereof brought forth ripe grapes:
verses.ct: "The vine had three branches. As soon as it sprouted, it blossomed, and its clusters produced ripe grapes."
verses.web: and in the vine were three branches. It was as though it budded, it blossomed, and its clusters produced ripe grapes.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh’s cup was in my hand: and I took the grapes, and pressed them into Pharaoh’s cup, and I gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand.
verses.ct: "I was holding Pharaoh's cup, so I took the grapes, squeezed them into Pharaoh's cup, and placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand."
verses.web: Pharaoh’s cup was in my hand; and I took the grapes, and pressed them into Pharaoh’s cup, and I gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand.”

verses.kjv: And Joseph said unto him, This is the interpretation of it: The three branches are three days:
verses.ct: Joseph told him, "Here is what it means: the three branches represent three days."
verses.web: Joseph said to him, “This is its interpretation: the three branches are three days.

verses.kjv: Yet within three days shall Pharaoh lift up thine head, and restore thee unto thy place: and thou shalt deliver Pharaoh’s cup into his hand, after the former manner when thou wast his butler.
verses.ct: "Within three days, Pharaoh will raise you up and give you back your position. You will once again put Pharaoh's cup in his hand, just as you used to do when you served as his cupbearer."
verses.web: Within three more days, Pharaoh will lift up your head, and restore you to your office. You will give Pharaoh’s cup into his hand, the way you did when you were his cup bearer.

verses.kjv: But think on me when it shall be well with thee, and shew kindness, I pray thee, unto me, and make mention of me unto Pharaoh, and bring me out of this house:
verses.ct: "But when things go well for you, please remember me. Show me kindness by mentioning me to Pharaoh and help get me out of this place."
verses.web: But remember me when it is well with you. Please show kindness to me, and make mention of me to Pharaoh, and bring me out of this house.

verses.kjv: For indeed I was stolen away out of the land of the Hebrews: and here also have I done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.
verses.ct: "I was taken by force from the land of the Hebrews, and even here I have done nothing to deserve being thrown into this dungeon."
verses.web: For indeed, I was stolen away out of the land of the Hebrews, and here also I have done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.”

verses.kjv: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was good, he said unto Joseph, I also was in my dream, and, behold, I had three white baskets on my head:
verses.ct: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, "I had a dream too! In it, I had three white baskets stacked on my head."
verses.web: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was good, he said to Joseph, “I also was in my dream, and behold, three baskets of white bread were on my head.

verses.kjv: And in the uppermost basket there was of all manner of bakemeats for Pharaoh; and the birds did eat them out of the basket upon my head.
verses.ct: "The top basket was filled with all kinds of baked goods for Pharaoh, but birds were eating them out of the basket on my head."
verses.web: In the uppermost basket there were all kinds of baked food for Pharaoh, and the birds ate them out of the basket on my head.”

verses.kjv: And Joseph answered and said, This is the interpretation thereof: The three baskets are three days:
verses.ct: Joseph responded, "Here is what it means: the three baskets represent three days."
verses.web: Joseph answered, “This is its interpretation. The three baskets are three days.

verses.kjv: Yet within three days shall Pharaoh lift up thy head from off thee, and shall hang thee on a tree; and the birds shall eat thy flesh from off thee.
verses.ct: "Within three days, Pharaoh will remove your head from your body and hang you on a tree, and the birds will eat your flesh."
verses.web: Within three more days, Pharaoh will lift up your head from off you, and will hang you on a tree; and the birds will eat your flesh from off you.”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass the third day, which was Pharaoh’s birthday, that he made a feast unto all his servants: and he lifted up the head of the chief butler and of the chief baker among his servants.
verses.ct: On the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, he made a feast for all his servants. He brought up the matter of the chief cupbearer and the chief baker before his servants.
verses.web: On the third day, which was Pharaoh’s birthday, he made a feast for all his servants, and he lifted up the head of the chief cup bearer and the head of the chief baker among his servants.

verses.kjv: And he restored the chief butler unto his butlership again; and he gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand:
verses.ct: He gave the chief cupbearer back his position, and the cupbearer once again placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.
verses.web: He restored the chief cup bearer to his position again, and he gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand;

verses.kjv: But he hanged the chief baker: as Joseph had interpreted to them.
verses.ct: But Pharaoh had the chief baker hanged — exactly as Joseph had explained to them.
verses.web: but he hanged the chief baker, as Joseph had interpreted to them.

verses.kjv: Yet did not the chief butler remember Joseph, but forgat him.
verses.ct: The chief cupbearer, however, did not remember Joseph. He forgot all about him.
verses.web: Yet the chief cup bearer didn’t remember Joseph, but forgot him.
